chapter_title.en: Chapter 19 – Embalming the deceased and his enshrouding

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (13/314)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (13/314)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) لِي فِي كَفْنِ أَبِي عُبَيْدَةَ الْحَذَّاءِ إِنَّمَا الْحَنُوطُ الْكَافُورُ وَ لَكِنِ اذْهَبْ فَاصْنَعْ كَمَا يَصْنَعُ النَّاسُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Salih Bin Al Sandy, from Ja’far Bin Bashir, from Dawood Bin Sirham who said,
‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said to me regarding the shroud of Abu Ubeyda Al-Haza’a: ‘But rather, the embalming (material) is the camphor, but go and do just as the people are doing’.
en.sarwar: 13. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Salih al-Sindiy from Ja‘far ibn Bashir from Dawud ibn Sarhan who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, once said to me about the shroud of abu ‘Ubaydah al-Hadhdha’, ‘Camphor is for hunut; however, go and do what people do.’”
